sources: NCSI via Muscat Daily (Jan 2025): 1,499,549 end-2024, 3% growth
NCSI via Oman Observer (Oct 2023): 6.1% national growth Q3 2023
NCSI via Oman Observer (Jan 2024): Muscat = 29.7% of national, 1,546,667
ONA (Aug 2024): 92 hospitals, 7,691 beds in Oman total
MoH Annual Health Report (2023): MoH manages 50 hospitals with 5,024 beds nationally (https://www.moh.gov.om)
WHO benchmark: 3.0 beds per 1,000 population (global median)
Times of Oman / Oman Observer (May 2023): 11 new hospitals under construction, including 400 planned beds for Muscat by 2028
Estimate: Muscat ~32% of total beds based on population share + capital concentration
Oman Observer (Aug 2024): 46 international schools, 61,704 students, 21 in Muscat
Benchmark: UNESCO recommends 15:1–20:1 student-teacher ratio; 15:1 used as quality target
Benchmark: 600 students/school quality target derived from UNESCO Education Indicators Technical Guidelines (uis.unesco.org) and World Bank school-size planning standards for urban areas
PAEW Annual Report 2023: Oman average domestic consumption around 180 liters/person/day (https://www.paew.gov.om — Annual Statistics section)
OPWP 7-Year Statement 2024–2030 (https://www.omanpwp.om): Barka (120 MLD), Al Ghubrah (191 MLD), and Sharqiyah (80 MLD) desalination capacities; Muscat effective supply estimated at ~280 MLD
Al Ghubrah 3 Independent Water Project: planned 300 MLD expansion from 2025 (OPWP procurement announcements, https://www.omanpwp.om)
WHO minimum domestic water access benchmark: 100 liters/person/day
Regional comparison: Gulf urban demand commonly 250-350 liters/person/day
